channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: US military flying surveillance aircraft for reconnaissance missions along southern border with Mexico
item.description: The U.S. military is conducting ISR missions via aircraft along the southern border under President Donald Trump's executive order addressing border security and immigration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Struggling with your mental health after surviving a disaster? Here's how to look after yourself
item.description: There’s the initial shock and chaos in a collective disaster like the deadly and destructive California wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Mariam Fam, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Purges at FBI, DOJ trigger 'battle' for career staff
item.description: The Trump administration has removed dozens of Department of Justice (DOJ) and FBI officials and is eyeing firing perhaps thousands more in an unprecedented purge that may just be getting started.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KRON4

item.title: China responds after Trump's tariffs take effect and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump oil tariffs expected to raise price at the pump
item.description: President Trump’s tariff on Canadian oil is expected to raise prices at the pump for U.S. consumers, if it takes effect as planned after a 30-day delay.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Major League Baseball fires ump amid gambling probe
item.description: Umpire Pat Hoberg was fired after it was revealed he shared betting accounts with a friend who bet on baseball.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KRON4

item.title: The man charged with stabbing Salman Rushdie will soon go on trial. The author will take the stand
item.description: The New Jersey man charged with severely injuring author Salman Rushdie in a 2022 knife attack is set to stand trial in New York.
item.dc:creator: Carolyn Thompson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trayon White channels Marion Barry as he fights corruption charges and expulsion from the DC Council
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Trayon White came up in local politics as a direct protege of iconic and infamous former Mayor Marion Barry. Now the D.C.
item.dc:creator: ASHRAF KHALIL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration opens antisemitism inquiries at 5 colleges including Columbia and Berkeley
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is opening new investigations into allegations of antisemitism at five U.S. universities including Columbia and the University of California, Berkeley, the Education Department announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration ends temporary deportation protection for 350,000 Venezuelans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's administration is ending protections that shielded roughly 350,000 Venezuelans from deportation, leaving them with two months before they lose their right to work in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA and GISELA SALOMON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Senate tees up final vote on Pam Bondi AG nomination
item.description: The Senate on Monday teed up a final vote on Pam Bondi's nomination to lead the Justice Department. The chamber voted 52-46 to limit debate. Absent an agreement with Democrats, that puts a final confirmation vote on the docket for the early hours of Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Regina Zilbermints, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk adds Shell, Nestlé, Lego to X advertiser 'boycott' lawsuit
item.description: X is adding several more companies to its lawsuit accusing firms of conspiring on an advertising "boycott" on the social media platform.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Salvage crews recover engine, large portion of jet from river after deadly air collision near DC
item.description: ARLINGTON, Va. (AP) — Salvage crews have recovered an engine and large pieces of fuselage and are working to retrieve a wing from the wreckage of a commercial airliner involved in last week's midair collision near Washington’s Reagan National Airport, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: DAVID R. MARTIN, MICHAEL R. SISAK and CLAUDIA LAUER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: ACLU sues over Trump shutting down asylum access at the southern border
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Immigration advocacy groups on Monday sued the Trump administration over its ban on asylum access at the southern border, saying the sweeping restrictions illegally put people who are fleeing war and persecution in harm's way.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Gerber recalls all batches of Soothe N Chew Teething Sticks nationwide
item.description: Gerber Products Company has announced a nationwide recall and discontinuation of all batches of its Soothe N Chew Teething Sticks due to a potential choking hazard for infants and young children.
item.dc:creator: Reagan Wynn, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tariff threats take aim at fentanyl trafficking. Here's how the drug reaches the US
item.description: President Donald Trump's plan to impose tariffs on goods from Mexico, Canada and China is partly aimed at combating the illicit flow of fentanyl into the U.S., where the opioid is blamed for some 70,000 overdose deaths annually. Mexico agreed Monday to send 10,000 troops to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Transportation secretary says 'expansive' impact zone was left by Philadelphia medical plane crash
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — The impact area where a medical transportation plane crashed in Philadelphia was notably “expansive,” U.S. Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy said Monday after viewing the site with investigators and city officials. Duffy said he was struck by “how expansive it was.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge orders acquittal of ex-prosecutor on 1 of 2 counts in misconduct trial over Ahmaud Arbery case
item.description: BRUNSWICK, Ga. (AP) — A judge on Monday ordered the acquittal of a former Georgia prosecutor on one of the two misconduct charges against her, ruling that prosecutors at her trial failed to present any evidence the she obstructed a police investigation of the 2020 killing of Ahmaud .Arbery.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Indianapolis man used fake deeds to sell 5 homes he didn't own
item.description: In addition to his 10-year federal prison sentence, James Henley, 35, was ordered to pay nearly $2 million in restitution.
item.dc:creator: Matt Christy, NewsNation

item.title: Trump signs order to create US sovereign wealth fund, eyeing TikTok purchase
item.description: President Trump on Monday directed secretaries to begin a process to create an American sovereign wealth fund, suggesting it could partially own the popular app TikTok.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: What USAID does, and why Trump and Musk want to get rid of it
item.description: In its first two weeks, President Donald Trump’s administration has made significant changes to the U.S. Agency for International Development.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer And Meg Kinnard, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Who gets more disaster aid? Republican states. Experts explain that and more about FEMA
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Federal disaster aid is nearly everywhere. About 94% of Americans live in a county that has gotten Federal Emergency Management Agency help for disasters since 2011.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Who gets more disaster aid? Republican states. Here's the breakdown
item.description: Experts say every state gets millions in federal help — and a little more goes to Republican states and congressional districts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Dozens of Education Department staffers on leave amid Trump's DEI purge
item.description: Dozens of employees with the Department of Education were placed on administrative leave on Friday, allegedly after attending a diversity training course encouraged by then-Secretary Betsy DeVos during the first Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: USAID leaders escorted out of building after blocking DOGE access to secure systems
item.description: Senior officials at the USAID were placed on administrative leave after they refused to turn over classified material.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Community gathers to remember life of Juan Acevedo
item.description: “You could tell he just had a really big heart. So I'm going to miss him,” said Juan’s English teacher.
item.dc:creator: Keagan Hughes, NewsNation

item.title: US Capitol Police recruit killed in Georgia car crash, other injured recruits airlifted to hospital
item.description: Democratic congressional leadership said U.S. Capitol Police Recruit Officer Christopher Katz died in a fatal car crash in Georgia that injured other recruits.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Army identifies 3rd soldier killed in DC crash
item.description: The U.S. Army on Saturday identified the third soldier who died last week during a training mission that ended in a midair crash between an Army Black Hawk helicopter and an American Airlines regional jet near Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9

item.title: American bald eagles are having a moment, ecologically and culturally
item.description: WEST ORANGE, N.J. (AP) — Along the long road from American icon to endangered species and back again, the bald eagle — the national bird of the United States, often seen against a clear blue sky — is having a moment.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jimmy Carter wins posthumous Grammy for narrating an audiobook of his Sunday school lessons
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Former President Jimmy Carter has won a posthumous Grammy award. Carter, the peanut farmer who won the presidency in the wake of the Watergate scandal and Vietnam War, died in December at age 100.
item.dc:creator: MARIA SHERMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Officials say remains of 55 of 67 victims of midair collision have been recovered and identified
item.description: ARLINGTON, Va. (AP) — Families of victims of the deadliest U.S. air disaster since 2001 visited the crash site on Sunday and divers scoured the submerged wreckage for more remains after authorities said they've recovered and identified 55 of the 67 people killed. Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: DAVID R. MARTIN, MICHAEL R. SISAK and CLAUDIA LAUER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: USAID security leaders on leave after trying to keep Musk's DOGE from classified info: officials
item.description: The two USAID security officials believed themselves legally obligated to deny access to Musk's DOGE crew since it lacked high-enough security clearance to access the information.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Grammy Awards: How to watch the ceremony, red carpet arrivals, interviews
item.description: The 67th annual Grammy Awards are set to recognize some of the most talented performers and biggest hits in pop music on Sunday, with different ways to watch the festivities.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Passengers, crew killed in 'high-impact' air ambulance crash in Philadelphia identified
item.description: Authorities said a seventh person, who had been in a car at the time of the crash, has died. Their identity has not yet been released.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: South Carolina retires jersey number of three-time WNBA MVP A’ja Wilson
item.description: Three-time WNBA MVP A’ja Wilson's South Carolina No. 22 jersey was hung in the rafters Sunday at Colonial Life Arena, her college home court that already features a statue of the program’s all-time leading scorer outside the main entrance.
item.dc:creator: PETE IACOBELLI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: China to file lawsuit with WTO, vows 'countermeasures' in response to Trump tariffs
item.description: In a statement, the ministry’s spokesperson vowed further “countermeasures” against the United States but did not specify what steps it would take.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC
